plugins.name: prd-workflow
plugins.description: End-to-end PRD pipeline: draft, evaluate gaps, brainstorm real-world situations/behaviours

plugins.name: strategic-research
plugins.description: Five-skill strategic research pipeline: industry process map → audience segments → willingness-to-pay → competitor evaluation → self-contained HTML synthesis report. Run end-to-end with /strategic-research or invoke any skill standalone.

plugins.name: pmm-define-and-review-positioning
plugins.description: Product positioning toolkit: guided 10-step workshop, positioning review/audit, competitive alternatives deep dive, market frame selection, and sales story builder. Run end-to-end with positioning-orchestrator or invoke any skill standalone.

plugins.name: user-research
plugins.description: End-to-end user research workflow: build and evaluate research briefs, create and evaluate interview guides, analyze research data, and review research analysis. Supports both general (discovery, validation, exploratory) and behavioral (COM-B, B=MAP) research methodologies.

plugins.name: plg-growth
plugins.description: Product-Led Growth toolkit: assess PLG readiness, decompose revenue drivers, choose acquisition models, run AARMS domain analysis (acquisition, activation, retention, monetisation, satisfaction), design growth loops, operationalize product-led sales, plan experiments, set up PLG data infrastructure, and plan org transformation. Grounded in structured problem solving (issue trees, hypothesis trees, MECE, 80/20, Minto Pyramid) and proven PLG frameworks.

plugins.name: event-tracking
plugins.description: Define, structure, and review analytics event tracking plans for any product. Four-stage pipeline: analytical use cases → event definition → tracking plan review → platform-specific formatting. Covers naming conventions, property design, user properties, firing conditions, and platform compliance for Amplitude, Mixpanel, PostHog, Segment, and GA4.

plugins.name: feature-flow
plugins.description: End-to-end feature delivery workflow for Claude Code: /ship takes a rough feature description through design → plan → approval gate → implement → verify → review/simplify → PR. Autonomous after plan approval or step-through, with subagent-driven, model-tiered implementers. A generic spine — per-repo behaviour (verify commands, test policy, stop-conditions, PR settings) is tuned via a local config file.

plugins.name: cro-engine
plugins.description: Conversion-rate-optimization reviewer: audits and improves landing pages, signup flows, paywalls, popups, onboarding, and forms against a library of CRO best-practice patterns and an expert-panel review gate.
